claims.evidence_text: the nitrogen requirement for a wheat crop yielding 2 t ha-1 of grain at 11 percent protein. It uses a correction factor of 2.34 ... e.g. 2 t ha-1 x 11 percent x correction factor = 52 kg N ha-1 required.
claims.claim_summary: A 2 t/ha wheat crop at 11% protein requires ~52 kg N/ha (protein x yield x 2.34)

claims.evidence_text: the later in crop growth that N is applied the greater is its impact on increasing protein and the less its proportional impact on biomass and yield. ... only part applied before or at sowing. ... A second tactical application to further increase protein can be made up to flowering.
claims.claim_summary: Split N timing in wheat: early N drives yield/biomass, later N (to flowering) drives grain protein

claims.evidence_text: you will be able to calculate N fertilizer requirements at sowing that will be accurate enough to meet targets of 70-80 percent of average yield assuming 11 percent grain protein.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat N budgeting commonly targets 11% grain protein

claims.evidence_text: As only about 50 percent of applied fertilizer is available in the first season ... actual recovery of nitrogen from soil supplies is often closer to 50 percent than 100 percent
claims.claim_summary: About 50% of applied N fertilizer is recovered by wheat in the first season
